title: Responsive image selection

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Responsive image selection; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Responsive image selection
sources.claim: srcset, sizes, and picture let the browser choose an appropriate image resource for density, viewport, format, or art direction.

sources.locator: MDN HTML Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Responsive image selection
sources.claim: srcset, sizes, and picture let the browser choose an appropriate image resource for density, viewport, format, or art direction.

canonicalExplanation.summary: srcset, sizes, and picture let the browser choose an appropriate image resource for density, viewport, format, or art direction.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: An img supplies width descriptors and an accurate sizes rule so a narrow layout downloads an appropriately sized source. Your goal is to implement and inspect responsive image selection for two viewport scenarios. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Relative and absolute URLs; Paragraph and inline meaning; Images and alternative text. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: srcset, sizes, and picture let the browser choose an appropriate image resource for density, viewport, format, or art direction. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Incorrect sizes estimates waste bandwidth, art direction differs from resolution switching, and every candidate still needs stable dimensions and alternatives. This draft therefore rejects: CSS width alone guarantees the browser downloads the smallest suitable image file.

includes: Use width descriptors, sizes and picture sources for resolution or art-direction needs.
Implement and inspect responsive image selection for two viewport scenarios.
excludes: Incorrect sizes estimates waste bandwidth, art direction differs from resolution switching, and every candidate still needs stable dimensions and alternatives.

outcomes: Implement and inspect responsive image selection for two viewport scenarios.

examples.title: Responsive image selection: concrete case
examples.situation: An img supplies width descriptors and an accurate sizes rule so a narrow layout downloads an appropriately sized source.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: An img supplies width descriptors and an accurate sizes rule so a narrow layout downloads an appropriately sized source.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Provide truthful srcset and sizes metadata; CSS layout size alone does not tell resource selection which file to fetch.
examples.result: The result follows because Provide truthful srcset and sizes metadata; CSS layout size alone does not tell resource selection which file to fetch.

examples.title: Responsive image selection: boundary case
examples.situation: Incorrect sizes estimates waste bandwidth, art direction differs from resolution switching, and every candidate still needs stable dimensions and alternatives.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Incorrect sizes estimates waste bandwidth, art direction differs from resolution switching, and every candidate still needs stable dimensions and alternatives.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: CSS width alone guarantees the browser downloads the smallest suitable image file.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Provide truthful srcset and sizes metadata; CSS layout size alone does not tell resource selection which file to fetch.
